reasoning: The informal statement asserts a Cauchy-Schwarz-like inequality for real sequences indexed 1 to n with coefficient j on the a-side and divisor j on the b-side. The Lean formalization uses Fin n indexing (0 to n-1) with coefficient (i + 1). Because the expected type of (i + 1) * a i ^ 2 is ℝ, Lean elaborates (i + 1) as (↑i : ℝ) + 1 where i coerces through Fin n → ℕ → ℝ, so (i + 1) ranges over {1, 2, ..., n} exactly matching j in the informal statement. Under the reindexing j = i.val + 1, the Lean inequality is identical to the informal one; dividing by (i + 1) also avoids division by zero since it starts at 1. Hypotheses (arbitrary real sequences a, b), conclusion, and quantifier structure all match.
